title: evidence-period output data
description:
Shape of the ``data`` field on an ``evidence-period`` envelope (E-verbs).

The window projection: the same conclusions / activity / envelopes /
citation-status / skip accounting as the brief, PLUS the
``unconcluded``-campaigns list that terminates the period render (the standing
invitation to close the conclusion loop). ``render`` rides for verbatim relay.

computed_at.description: The instant the digest was computed against (ISO-8601 UTC).
computed_at.title: Computed At

as_of.description: The window's upper cut (until), echoed; null when open-ended.
as_of.title: As Of

conclusions.description: Dated, sha-cited conclusions in the window — newest first.
conclusions.title: Conclusions

activity.description: Per-tag prior-work counts within the window.
activity.title: Activity

envelopes.description: Per-lineage determinism envelopes recorded within the window (ledger-verbatim).
envelopes.title: Envelopes

unconcluded.description: Terminal campaigns in the window with NO conclusion naming them (the loop-closing list).
unconcluded.title: Unconcluded

skipped.description: Namespaces skipped during fleet collection (fail-open accounting).
skipped.title: Skipped

cache.description: hit | miss | disabled — the cache disposition for this read.
cache.title: Cache

render.description: The deterministic markdown digest — relayed to the human verbatim.
render.title: Render

ActivityLine.description:
Per-tag prior-work counts — identity + counting, never a judgment (E-render).

Renders 'PRIOR WORK · 3 campaigns, 14 runs, 2 lineages · newest 2025-11-02 ·
9 looks on <tag>'. Every field is a COUNT or a DATE read from the records'
own fields — no ranking, no urgency.

tag.description: The scope-tag slug this activity is counted under (opaque caller data).
tag.title: Tag

campaigns.description: Count of campaigns matched under this tag.
campaigns.title: Campaigns

runs.description: Count of runs matched under this tag.
runs.title: Runs

lineages.description: Count of distinct command-identity lineages matched.
lineages.title: Lineages

looks.description: Count of prior looks recorded on this tag's look ledger.
looks.title: Looks

newest.description: ISO-8601 ts of the newest matched record under this tag (null when none).
newest.title: Newest

ActivityLine.title: evidence per-tag activity line

CitationStatusLine.description:
Per-citation re-resolution disclosure at READ (E-shape: disclose, never refuse).

Evidence legitimately moves after a conclusion is recorded (archived to S3, a
store re-exported, a repo wiped). The read side re-resolves each citation and
reports ``verified`` / unresolvable per line — the conclusion stays a truthful
dated record; the drift is disclosed, the reader decides. (The APPEND gate is
the deliberate exception where an unresolvable citation REFUSES — that never
runs here.)

conclusion_id.description: The conclusion this citation belongs to (opaque slug).
conclusion_id.title: Conclusion Id

kind.description: The citation's mechanism kind (closed set) — its one resolver.
kind.title: Kind

ref.description: The opaque citation ref (run_id / cmd_sha key / dossier path); echoed, never parsed.
ref.title: Ref

sha.description: The full sha the conclusion recorded for this citation.
sha.title: Sha

verified.description: True = re-resolved and the sha still matches here; False = unresolvable/mismatched at read time.
verified.title: Verified

CitationStatusLine.title: evidence citation-status line

ConclusionLine.description:
One conclusion in the digest — a dated, sha-cited, human-authored finding.

The lead of the point query (newest current conclusion first). ``finding`` is
opaque caller prose: stored, rendered VERBATIM, never parsed. ``cited_shas``
echoes the sha prefixes the record rests on (the evidence-bound rule);
``status`` is the reduced conclusion status.

conclusion_id.description: The conclusion's caller-authored slug (opaque path segment).
conclusion_id.title: Conclusion Id

ts.description: The finding's date (the record's ts) — every line dated.
ts.title: Ts

tags.description: The conclusion's own tags (retro-indexing key); opaque caller data.
tags.title: Tags

cited_shas.description: Evidence sha prefixes the conclusion cites (the evidence-bound rule); echoed, never parsed.
cited_shas.title: Cited Shas

status.description: current | superseded | revoked | absent — the reduced conclusion status.
status.title: Status

finding.description: The human's free-text finding — opaque, rendered verbatim, never interpreted.
finding.title: Finding

ConclusionLine.title: evidence conclusion line

EnvelopeLine.description:
One lineage's determinism envelope, quoting the fingerprint ledger VERBATIM.

Renders 'ENVELOPE · lineage 7be4… · ±2.1% rel (n=4: 3 full + 1 partial,
scales: main, clusters: hoffman2)'. ``envelope`` is the ledger's own rendered
reduction (never recomputed or reinterpreted here); the evidence-label block
``{n, n_full, n_partial, scales, clusters}`` is quoted verbatim from the
fingerprint ledger (``docs/design/determinism-fingerprint.md``).

lineage.description: The command-identity lineage key (cmd_sha) this envelope is for.
lineage.title: Lineage

envelope.description: The ledger's own rendered envelope (e.g. '±2.1% rel'); quoted, never recomputed.
envelope.title: Envelope

n.description: Total sample count (full + partial) — the ledger's evidence label.
n.title: N

n_full.description: Full-scale (non-partial) sample count — verbatim from the ledger.
n_full.title: N Full

n_partial.description: Partial-scale sample count — verbatim from the ledger.
n_partial.title: N Partial

scales.description: Scale labels present in the ledger's evidence block (opaque labels, verbatim).
scales.title: Scales

clusters.description: Cluster labels present in the ledger's evidence block (opaque labels, verbatim).
clusters.title: Clusters

EnvelopeLine.title: evidence envelope line

SkippedNamespace.description:
One namespace skipped during fleet collection, with the reason (fail-open).

A wiped repo, an unreadable/torn ``repo.json``, or an absent store must never
crash the read — it is skipped silently and counted here (the attention-queue
``SkippedNamespace`` posture, mirrored).

ref.description: The repo_hash / namespace id that was skipped.
ref.title: Ref

reason.description: Why it was skipped (unreadable, torn, absent).
reason.title: Reason

SkippedNamespace.title: evidence skipped namespace

UnconcludedItem.description:
One terminal campaign with NO conclusion naming it (E-render; period only).

Pure IDENTITY matching (a terminal campaign whose id appears in no
conclusion's ``concludes`` set), never text matching. Dated by its completion
ts so the item AGES honestly — the standing invitation to close the loop. It
is a LIST entry, never a verdict: a missing conclusion blocks nothing (E3).

scope_kind.description: The subject kind (campaign / run / scope) with no conclusion naming it.
scope_kind.title: Scope Kind

scope_id.description: The subject id (opaque; the identity the unconcluded predicate matched).
scope_id.title: Scope Id

completed_at.description: The subject's completion-brief ts — the instant the item ages from.
completed_at.title: Completed At

UnconcludedItem.title: evidence unconcluded campaign item
